prose:
**Pyridine Fukui Reactivity Indices** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier `standard`, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral; cation +1 mult. 2 and anion −1 mult. 2 computed for state differentiation). Geometry: user-supplied (`pyridine.xyz`, 11 atoms). Partial charges via Mulliken analysis. SCF converged cleanly for all three electronic states; charge-conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). **Most electrophilic site (atom 1, N): f⁺ = 0.48422572**. **Most nucleophilic site (atom 4, C): f⁻ = 0.1182626**. Fukui indices (electrophilic f⁺, nucleophilic f⁻, radical f⁰, dual descriptor) computed for all 11 atoms via the standard finite-difference protocol (cation − neutral charges for f⁺; neutral − anion charges for f⁻). Nitrogen dominates electrophilic reactivity; ortho/meta carbons show modest nucleophilic character. Full atom-by-atom indices reported above. Plot generated. Command: `fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`. Integrity check: `trustworthy: true`.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.48422572
